title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Pelham, NY | $900 – $2,500 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Pelham, Westchester. Humane bat extraction, attic cleanup, and permanent exclusion. Licensed experts. Call today!
intro: Dealing with bats in your Pelham home or business? Best Pest Control NYC provides comprehensive bat removal and exclusion services throughout Pelham, Westchester County. Our licensed technicians safely remove bats and seal entry points to prevent re-infestation, protecting your property and family from health risks.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has been serving Pelham and Westchester County for years with proven bat exclusion methods that comply with New York wildlife regulations. Our experienced team uses humane removal techniques and provides thorough attic remediation to ensure your home remains bat-free permanently.
localContext: Pelham's historic homes and tree-lined neighborhoods near Pelham Bay Park and the Manor area are particularly susceptible to bat colonization in attics and eaves. The charming Tudor and Colonial-style residences common throughout Pelham Manor and Pelham proper often feature the architectural gaps and ventilation systems that bats exploit for roosting.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Pelham, NY?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Pelham typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, extent of attic damage, and the complexity of your home's architecture. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in Pelham and Westchester County?
faqs.answer: Yes, but bat removal must be performed humanely and in compliance with New York State wildlife regulations. Bats cannot be removed during maternity season (approximately May through August) when flightless young are present. Our licensed professionals ensure all work meets legal requirements and uses exclusion methods that allow bats to leave but not return.

faqs.question: What are signs of bats in my Pelham home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds in your attic at dusk or dawn, visible bat droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, dark staining around eaves or vents from body oils, and seeing bats enter or exit your home at twilight. You may also notice a strong ammonia-like odor from accumulated guano.

faqs.question: How long does the bat exclusion process take?
faqs.answer: The complete bat exclusion process typically takes 2-4 weeks in Pelham homes. After the initial inspection, we install one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to exit but not re-enter. Once we confirm all bats have left (usually 3-7 days), we permanently seal all entry points and can perform attic cleanup and sanitization to remove guano and restore insulation if needed.
